title: D-Orbit signs launch-services contract with ArkEdge Space
summary: D-Orbit, an Italian space logistics company, has signed a launch-services contract with Japanese startup ArkEdge Space to provide launches using its ION Satellite Carrier. The agreement was announced on July 8.

title: Orbit Fab hires new CEO and raises funding to support satellite refueling business
summary: Orbit Fab has appointed a new CEO and secured additional funding to advance its satellite refueling operations. The company aims to shift from technology development to commercial services.

title: Spirit Electronics Announces Managed Access to U.S.-Based Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing for Aerospace and Defense Programs
summary: Spirit Electronics introduced a managed-access program to provide aerospace and defense customers with secure access to U.S.-based advanced semiconductor manufacturing. The initiative aims to support domestic production for critical space and defense applications.

title: Apolink makes contact with first relay satellite
summary: Apolink successfully established contact with its first relay satellite launched via SpaceX's rideshare mission on July 7. This milestone enables a data relay demonstration under an experimental FCC license.

title: Arianespace opens door to GEO rideshare opportunities from 2029
summary: Arianespace has partnered with Infinite Orbits to explore rideshare launch services to geostationary orbit (GEO) starting in 2029. The collaboration aims to expand GEO access through shared missions.

title: SpaceX launches Transporter-17 amid concerns about rideshare program’s future
summary: SpaceX launched the Transporter-17 rideshare mission on July 7, amid growing industry concerns about the program's sustainability. Rivals have described the situation as a 'panic' regarding the future of rideshare missions.

title: Simera Sense to provide imagers for Canary Islands constellation
summary: Simera Sense, a Belgian company, secured a contract to supply multispectral imagers for a small constellation aimed at monitoring wildfires, volcanic activity, and other regional priorities in the Canary Islands. The constellation is designed to support environmental and disaster management efforts in the region.

title: SpaceX satellite wins test Pentagon’s commitment to competition
summary: SpaceX's satellite contract has prompted Pentagon scrutiny over its dominance in military satellite networks. Congress is pushing for increased competition in the sector.

title: Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site
summary: Isar Aerospace has signed an agreement to establish a Canadian launch site for its Spectrum rocket, which is tied to a submarine deal with the Canadian military.

title: Blue Origin continues work on lunar landers during recovery from New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin is progressing with the development of its Blue Moon lunar landers, having seven vehicles in production, despite recovering from the recent New Glenn rocket explosion. The company remains focused on its lunar lander program amid the incident.

title: James Webb Space Telescope celebrates its 4th birthday with stunning image of a galaxy crash site
summary: NASA released a stunning image of the galaxy Centaurus A, a cosmic collision site from 2 billion years ago, to celebrate the James Webb Space Telescope's 4th anniversary.

title: Our Milky Way galaxy might be larger than we thought
summary: Astronomers discovered that the Milky Way galaxy might be larger than previously thought by analyzing cosmic explosions. This challenges existing assumptions about the galaxy's size.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is finally streaming Amazon Prime Video, and we can't wait to watch it again
summary: The sci-fi movie 'Project Hail Mary' is now available for streaming on Amazon Prime Video starting July 3. Fans are excited to rewatch the film.

title: Artemis 2's Jeremy Hansen stepping down from active astronaut duty after epic moon mission
summary: Jeremy Hansen, the Canadian astronaut assigned to NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission, will step down from active astronaut duty in September to take on a new role. The Canadian Space Agency announced this transition following his participation in the historic lunar mission.

title: How public–private partnerships can turn Mars into an economic frontier (Op-Ed)
summary: A public–private partnership is essential for developing the technologies needed for sustained Mars settlement, as no single agency can achieve this alone.

title: China releases 1st photo of Earth's elusive 'quasi-moon' Kamo'oalewa
summary: China's Tianwen-2 probe captured the first photo of Earth's quasi-moon Kamo'oalewa, also known as asteroid 2016HO3. This marks a significant milestone in China's space exploration efforts.

title: 'That's going to come back and bite us': Former NASA chief questions Artemis moon lander plans
summary: Former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine has raised concerns about the designs for NASA's commercial moon landers under the Artemis program, warning that flaws could lead to future problems.

title: Astronaut flexes his muscles mid-spacewalk | Space photo of the day for July 7, 2026
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams flexed his muscles during a spacewalk with Jessica Meir on July 7, 2026. The photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day.

title: Chinese scientists find the best way to nuke an asteroid on its way to impact Earth
summary: Chinese scientists have proposed two methods to deflect an asteroid on a collision course with Earth. The research aims to prepare for potential future threats despite no imminent danger.

title: SpaceX just launched the 1st-ever nuclear-powered commercial satellite
summary: SpaceX launched the first-ever commercial satellite powered by nuclear energy as part of its Transporter-17 rideshare mission. This marks a significant milestone in the use of nuclear propulsion for commercial space applications.

title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
summary: NASA transferred ownership of 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center to the Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The land, previously known as Area 400, is now part of the largest unfragmented forest block in the region.

title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft has woken from its longest hibernation period of nearly a year in good health and is preparing to transmit science data from the Kuiper Belt. Flight controllers at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory confirmed the spacecraft's status on June 23.

title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
summary: The Artemis II crew dedicated a second-generation Apollo 14 Moon tree at NASA's Johnson Space Center in 2026. The loblolly pine tree is part of a legacy of seeds flown to the Moon during the Apollo 14 mission.

title: July 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: A satellite image from July 2026 is presented as a puzzle, challenging viewers to identify its location and explain its significance. The image is noted for its unique or intriguing features that make it interesting.

title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station have captured photographs of several host cities for the 2026 FIFA World Cup from 250 miles above Earth. This highlights the unique perspective of space-based observations of major global events.

title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover in a personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026. The event celebrates America's 250th anniversary and highlights the nation's historical achievements in exploration and spaceflight.

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations
summary: NASA released a draft Request for Proposals (RFP) seeking industry feedback on the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy to ensure a smooth transition of low Earth orbit activities from the ISS. The move aims to gather input from American companies for future space station development.

title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
summary: NASA's CAPSTONE mission has completed its extended mission to test lunar technologies, advancing autonomous navigation and communication capabilities for future Moon missions. The spacecraft validated critical systems for sustained human presence on the Moon.

title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope reveals a complex galaxy transformation in new images, uncovering details previously obscured by dust. The telescope's infrared sensitivity highlights the galaxy Centaurus A's intricate structure shaped by cosmic collisions.

title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
summary: Researchers used satellite data to link harmful algal blooms at Blue Mesa Reservoir to warm water and low water levels. The study highlights environmental impacts on one of Colorado's largest reservoirs.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in commercial space habitation.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is advancing preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, Florida, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are accelerating efforts to meet this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar landers for its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. The contracts aim to support the development of infrastructure for sustained lunar exploration.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help scientists study the galaxy's structure and evolution.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate visitors about the shuttle's history and contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25 decisions with industry stakeholders. These events will outline planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The news discusses a significant advancement in robotic arm technology for space applications. This innovation is expected to enhance various space operations and missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young scientists, test materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for mission success.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to advance satellite licensing reform with a vote on the Space Modernization Order scheduled for July 22. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision last week.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to LEO, including the first commercial nuclear satellite.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to replace the ISS with stand-alone commercial habitats in LEO after receiving industry feedback on human habitation. The agency has released a draft Request for Proposal (RFP) for the Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) program.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. The company aims to make laser communications more accessible with lighter, smaller, and scalable solutions.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article highlights the largest fundraises in the first half of 2026 within the space industry. It emphasizes how these investments are enabling advancements in rocket technology and payload capacity.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have fostered stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the global space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC. The mission highlights True Anomaly's rapid response capabilities in space imaging.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, named Aurora. This mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge announced plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The approach aims to leverage local lunar dust for building support structures and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA praised Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and announced contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims for monthly landings with NASA payloads using updated lander designs.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
summary: CesiumAstro has filed an FCC application for a 737-satellite constellation named Synchronicity to offer reconfigurable connectivity for fixed and mobile users. The company, based in Austin, Texas, specializes in software-defined satellite solutions.

title: NATO Allies Plan Hybrid HALO Constellation
summary: Eight NATO allies plan to integrate national military satellites into a hybrid constellation called HALO to enhance NATO's space resilience and military advantage.

title: SpaceX’s Transporter-17 Mission Launches 81 Payloads
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 81 payloads aboard its Transporter-17 rideshare mission using a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base. The mission took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Space Systems Command Awards SES 5-Year Blanket Purchase Agreement
summary: The U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command has awarded SES Space and Defense a five-year blanket purchase agreement for global Ku-band connectivity services. This agreement includes managed connectivity solutions.

title: An Expedition Across the Arctic-Space Connection With David Marsh
summary: David Marsh's expedition to the Arctic aims to draw parallels between the hostile environment of the Arctic and space. The findings from his journey are discussed in an episode of 'On Orbit'.

title: Neo Space and Hughes Deploy 3 Ground Gateways to Support IFC
summary: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems have deployed three new Hughes ground gateways in Saudi Arabia and Europe to support NSG's in-flight connectivity services.
company: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems

title: Univity Signs MoU With Indonesia’s Telkomsat on VLEO and D2D Connectivity
summary: Univity and Indonesia's Telkomsat signed an MoU to collaborate on satellite connectivity, focusing on Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) architecture and direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity.

title: Mission to Rescue NASA Swift Observatory Launches
summary: A mission to extend the life of NASA's Swift Observatory has launched, with the Katalyst Space LINK servicing spacecraft successfully contacted. Northrop Grumman executed the launch.

title: Cyber in Space Signs Partnership Deal with ExeQuantum
summary: Cyber in Space has partnered with ExeQuantum to enhance cyber resilience and quantum-readiness services for the global aerospace and space ecosystem. The collaboration aims to deliver advanced security solutions to organizations in the space industry.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service after a successful ULA launch. This marks a significant milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.
